question: Pipe A is capable of filling a tank in 16 minutes while Pipe B can fill the same tank in 24 minutes. Both pipes are opened together, but after 4 minutes, the rate of Pipe A doubles and the rate of Pipe B becomes half of its original. How many more minutes will it take to fill the tank completely?

Pipes A and B can fill a tank in 20 minutes and 30 minutes, respectively. Meanwhile, a drainpipe C can empty the tank in 60 minutes. If all three pipes are opened simultaneously, how long will it take to fill the tank?

Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 18 and 24 minutes respectively. After both are opened together for 6 minutes, the rate of pipe A becomes half, and pipe B becomes double its original. In how many more minutes will the tank be full?

A man and a woman are together filling a tank with water. The man pours 4 liters of water every 3 minutes, while the woman pours 3 liters every 4 minutes. How much time will it take to fill 100 liters of water in the tank?
A tap can fill an oil tank in 14 hours. After half the tank is filled, 7 more similar taps are opened. What is the total time taken to fill the oil tank completely?
Two taps can fill a tub in 6 minutes and 4 minutes, respectively. A pipe can empty it in 3 minutes. If all the three are kept open simultaneously, when will the tub be full?
A tank is full of water. If 20 liters of water is added, it becomes full. What is the full capacity of the tank? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 3]

A tank has two pipes, one can fill it with water in 14 hours and the other can empty it in 8 hours. In how many hours will the tank be emptied if both the pipes are opened together when 1/4th of the tank is already filled with water? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift- 2]

Pipe A, B and C together can fill a tank in 9 minutes. Pipe B and C together can fill the same tank in 12 minutes. Pipe C and A together can fill the same tank in 18 minutes. In how much time (in minutes) pipe B alone can fill the tank?

Pipe A and B together can fill a tank in 16 minutes. Pipe B and C together can fill the same tank in 20 minutes. Pipe C and A together can fill the same tank in 24 minutes. Firstly pipe C is opened. After 'L' minutes, pipes A and B both are also opened. Remaining tank got filled in 4 minutes. What is the value of L?

Pipe A and B together can fill a tank in 12 minutes. Pipe B and C together can fill the same tank in 15 minutes. Pipe C and A together can fill the same tank in 20 minutes. Firstly pipe C is opened. After 'L' minutes, pipes A and B both are also opened. Remaining tank got filled in 5 minutes. What is the value of L?

Pipe A, B and C together can fill a tank in 20 minutes. Pipe B and C together can fill the same tank in 30 minutes. Pipe C and A together can fill the same tank in 24 minutes. In how much time (in minutes) pipe B alone can fill the tank?
A pipe can fill a tank in 2 2/3 hours, and another pipe empties it in 4 hours. If both are opened together, how long will it take to fill the tank?
A water pipe supplies water at a constant rate. In 4.5 minutes, it fills 2 liters. At this rate, how much time will it take to fill 5.625 liters?
A water tank is filled th in 15 minutes. How long to fill the full tank?
A pipe can fill a tank in 100 minutes. In how much time (in seconds) can it fill 80 percent of the tank?
A pipe can empty part of a tank in 36 minutes. In how much time (in minutes) will it empty the complete tank?
A pipe can empty a tank in 20 hours. What part of this tank will it empty in 5 hours?
A pipe can empty 1/4 part of a tank in 8 minutes. In how much time (in minutes) can it empty 1/2 part of that tank?
A pipe can empty 1/3 part of a tank in 45 minutes. In how much time (in minutes) will it empty the complete tank?
A pipe can empty a tank in 48 minutes. In how much time (in minutes) it can empty part of this tank?
A pipe can empty a tank in 36 hours. What part of this tank will it empty in 6 hours?
A group of 8 pumps can fill a tank in 15 hours. After working for 5 hours, 4 more pumps are added. Find the total time required to fill the tank.
A pipe can empty a tank in 36 minutes. In how much time (in minutes) it can empty 1/4 part of this tank?

Pipes P1 and P2 alone can fill a tank in 5 hours and 10 hours respectively. Pipe P3 alone can empty the same tank in 20 hours. Firstly, only pipes P1 and P2 are opened and after 1 hour pipe P3 is also opened. In how much total time (in hours) tank will be completely filled?
A pipe can fill a tank in 6 hours, another pipe can fill it in 8 hours, but a leak can empty it in 12 hours. If all work together, how long will it take to fill the tank?

A water tank can be filled by two inlets, labeled A and B, in 12 hours and 15 hours respectively. An outlet pipe C can empty the tank in 10 hours. If all three pipes are opened together, how much time will it take to fill the tank?
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (12th), 25 Jul 2025, Shift 2

question: Pipe A can fill a tank in 10 hours, Pipe B in 20 hours. Pipe C can empty the full tank in 25 hours. If all are opened together, how long will it take to fill the tank?
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (12th), 29 Aug 2025, Shift 2

question: A water tanker can hold four-fifth of its capacity when three-fourth of the water is already used. If the tanker's total capacity is 3600 litres, how many litres of water is left in the water tanker?
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (12th), 29 Aug 2025, Shift 2

question: Pipe A can fill an empty tank in 18 hours and pipe B in 24 hours. If both the pipes are opened and after 3 hours pipe A is closed, how | much time (in hrs) B will take to fill the remaining tank?
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (12th), 31 Jul 2025, Shift 2

question: A tank holds 27.5 liters of water. If a leak causes 3/8 of the water to drain out, how much water remains in the tank (in liters)? ⚠️ links will not give you any mock test. Please use only the original PDF.
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (10th), 28 Jul 2025, Shift 1
